item_specifications.special_feature: Adjustable Shoulder Strap, Airline Approved, Expandable, Foldable, Softsided

about_item: ↓↓【Something You Need To Read Before Purchase】→→ 1.Recommended size :【extra-small dog/small cat shorter than 16'',weight is up to 5 lbs】Please measure your pet length firstly,length is more important than weight to judge a pet can be carried or not. Make sure your pet is shorter than 16 inches. This carrier is designed specially for frontier /allegiant airlines,the height is very low(only 8 inches), SO NOT Recommend for a oversize pet.

2.【Designed for frontier/allegiant/breeze airlines】 size of carrier is 17''(L) x 13''(W) x 8''(H),it can be putted under the seat in cabin,it conform to the requirement of frontier / Allegiant airlines (18''x14''x8''),it also is Southwest / Spirit approved,but it is not the first choice for these airlines(9'' height carrier is a better choice),IT IS NOT APPROVED BY DELTA\UNITED AMERICAN ALASKA (13'' is more wider ).

3.【Expandable Top provide more space for your pet】 This carrier have a 3'' height expandable top(fully extended),you can fold it down to put carrier under seat in cabin and pull it up to giving more space for pet in other travel time.Not like other similar products, expandable top in our product have a 5 sides meshes to provide better Air mobility and 360 degrees visual field for your pet.

4.【Stable and safety】 2 hard metal frames in 2 sides to keep stable and prevent collapse(frames only has a little elasticity to keep shape well,them can not bend to a lot lower height),a hard wood padding to prevent bottom sinking. Double zipper lock,anti-scratch mesh and a tether in carrier to prevent pet escaping.

5.【A carrier for multi use,match your needs in travel】 this carrier can be used for other airlines that have a more than 8 inches height under-seat,it also work in a short distance car trip,like vet visit.Adjustable shoulder strap make carrying easily,2 pockets with zippers for storing objects for your pet in travel,soft padding in bottom is washable.A adaptive training(load your pet into carrier and keep it away from anxiety) for your pet before travel is important.
6.【1 year warranty policy】We provide a one-year warranty for the quality of our product.Included zippers uckleshooks abric.
【Pet Size For】1 extra-small dog or small cat. 【MAX Pet Length】16 inches. 【MAX Pet Weight】5 lbs. 【Approved Airline】Allegiant and Frontier.

summary.text: Customers find the pet carrier spacious enough for two smaller kittens and perfect for flying with Frontier and Allegiant airlines. The carrier is sturdy, comfortable, and fits great under Allegiant airlines seats, with plenty of pockets and good interior space. They appreciate the quality construction and the zippers with clips that make it easy to access their pets.

authors_reviews.title: Allegiant compliant
authors_reviews.text: The 17x13x8 was perfect for an Allegiant flight from FL to IL. My old girl is a 9lb cat and had plenty of room to move about. The top portion can be pushed down to go under the seat while in the cabin, which is a nice function to have. The 2-hour flight was great, and this carrier was also wonderful for an 8-hour car drive from FL to SC. My cat tends to travel everywhere with me by car, so I bought this carrier specifically for an Allegiant flight, but it is her go-to carrier now. The size was compliant, roomy, and I like that there is a leash clasp for a harness within the carrier, which was nice to have for TSA. It has multiple openings that can also be buckle-locked, and two pockets for carrying paperwork, a leash, and her collar. Overall, I love this carrier's design. It's a great carrier for your money and I definitely recommend it.
authors_reviews.author: J.Harris

authors_reviews.title: Functional and sturdy - it's pom perfect!
authors_reviews.text: This is absolutely the best carrier! I was so nervous my Pomeranian would be too cramped on the flight, but the expanding sections gave her plenty of room. It's great quality and was sturdy as I carried it around the airport, but had enough flexibility that I could manipulate the edges past the back-of-seat outlets. I also love that the zippers are clips, so I could easily attach her water bowl.Overall really exceptional product, but I do wish it had more/larger pockets.
authors_reviews.author: Brando

authors_reviews.title: Well made and convenient dog carrier
authors_reviews.text: This is the perfect dog bag for airplane and car rides. I like all the extra pockets for treats and poo bags. I like the different opening options on the top. It's sturdy and fits under plane seat then I can strap it in the car. Great price too. My dog is 12 pounds, a little maltipoo. It's a perfect size for him.
authors_reviews.author: Jennifer D

authors_reviews.title: Dog escaped
authors_reviews.text: A word of caution, I purchased this carrier recently. It is very well made and sturdy. Easily fits under an extra leg room seat. However, either through user error, or zipper lock malfunction, my pup got out of the carrier on the plane. The design has multiple openings with zippers and locks. Plastic, rather flimsy locks, imo. But, I could have missed a lock, so maybe no fault of the design. My pup was corralled by the FA after a visit with another passenger. I was thanked for providing in flight entertainment. It turned out ok. No harm done, except for my total embarrassment. So, please double check that all the locks are engaged.
authors_reviews.author: lorraine hancock

authors_reviews.title: Perfect pet carrier for traveling
authors_reviews.text: We absolutely love this pet carrier. Being able to wheel our dog through the airport made traveling so much easier than having to carry him. As such, it has pockets for snacks and other small items that may be needed during the trip. The only downside to this is that the top of the carrier did not hold its shape well and would collapse a little which was annoying but def not a deal breaker. We used this to fly Allegiant airlines out of Punta Gorda, FL. and there were no issues at all. Fit under the seat nicely. 100% recommend.
authors_reviews.author: Jeremiah Soto

authors_reviews.title: Very nice design but small end entry and awkward trolley sleeve. Allegient compliant, BUT…
authors_reviews.text: I really like the design of this carrier for allegiant airlines size requirements. I enjoy that the top expands so that the dog can turn around in the bag and pass the turnaround test if the check-in people make you do that. I have had them make me do that once…I have a 22 inch four wheel rolling, hard side, carry-on bag, and I can set this bag on top using the trolley sleeve, but it’s a little awkward and you really need to use the top straps to provide leverage by putting them over the handle on the rolling bag or you need to loop the long strap around the handle of the rolling bag to prevent it from tipping with the dog inside it. You will see what I mean when you start to use it. I do like the very firm base inside the bag.REMEMBER THAT IF YOU ARE TAKING A PET CARRIER ON A PLANE YOU STILL ONLY GET TWO CARRY ON BAGS (ONE IN THE OVERHEAD AND A SMALLER ONE UNDER THE SEAT) THIS IS A USA FEDERAL THING, NOT AN AIRLINE THING. SO THE BAG COUNTS AS ONE OF THE TWO EVEN THOUGH YOU PAID A PREMIUM TO TAKE IT ON. PREPARE TO CONSOLIDATE OR CHECK THE EXTRA BAG.Most airlines let you count it as your personal item because it goes under the seat, but you better check.Normally I want my dogs to go in and out the side of the bag. The end of the rectangle side that will face you when you are in the airplane (like they are going in and out of a dog kennel) but this bag has a really small panel on that side to let the dog in and out. I don’t really like that about it. I would prefer that that were a bigger panel, but I can see why it isn’t because the bag has metal reinforcement on the sides and I don’t think it’s possible to make that panel bigger. So, I have to put my dog in the top, which is OK,especially with the expanding top.The bag that I use on bigger planes does not have a top entry (sturdibag).My middle sized Boston terrier is about 15 pounds and she is kind of long but she fits in the bag just fine and she can stand up (without lifting her head completely) turn around and lay down comfortably. Under the seat with the top popped down, she can turn around if she crouches and scoots… but I pull her out from under the seat and check on her frequently so she can turn around and stretch if she wants. My 19 lb Boston doesn’t fit well enough for comfort. She can fit in the bag and turn around with the top popped up but she can’t stand up. She has to stay lying down or crouching. She would probably pass the check in people if they didn’t inspect her too closely, but I wouldn’t chance it. Plus it is not nice to her… at all. My tiny 10 lb Boston is no problem. She can run loops in there.Someone wrote that they did not like the carrier because it did not fit under business/first class seats. That is a common problem with all business class seats. They are quite low to the ground, but typically they are not going to kick you out of business or the plane if your carrier fits their standard size requirements for the coach seats. They will just allow you to hold it in your lap or something until the plane takes off and then you could put it on the ground by your feet until landing time. (Been there and done that with the much bigger but more squishy sturdibag). Depends on the flight attendants of course…. Yes, holding it in your lap is probably a big no no but what else are you going to do? You can’t put an animal in the overhead… dogs have died that way. So as long as it meets the airline size requirements, I am sure they will work with you in first class. Plus you can hold babies in your lap… and not pay for them…. Hmmmm.For Allegient, I highly recommend a middle seat or extra leg room seats. See my photos. The photo with the carrier in the middle seat is taken in the extra legroom seat section. The photo with my feet visible is taken in the extra (premium legroom) legroom seat section in the window seat and the seat where you can’t hardly see anything is a regular seat. You will have zero legroom if you sit in that seat with this carrier, Yet somehow it is still compliant. If you have to sit in a regular seat or you don’t pay extra, please choose a middle seat. You will be able to turn the carrier sideways as seen in the pictures and it will fit perfectly.
authors_reviews.author: Benegesserit

authors_reviews.title: PIDIPITA: I wish this carrier was 1 inch longer and 1 inch wider.
authors_reviews.text: I had to buy this carrier because Alligent airlines decided that the carrier I'd been using for years, on their planes, was suddenly too big. 😡This is a sturdy carrier. Although my fur baby barely fits, it's wide and she can at least lay on her side and sleepThe pop up top could be a bit bigger for taller dogs.Allegiant's requirement is no bigger than 18x14x8.PIDIPITA: I wish this carrier was 1 inch longer and 1 inch wider.This is a good little carrier for small dogs under 10lbs
authors_reviews.author: Margaret S. Turner

other_countries_reviews.title: Dimensions
other_countries_reviews.text: Nice sturdy carrier and fits guideline dimensions for under seat
other_countries_reviews.author: James gang

other_countries_reviews.title: Meets the Air Canada requirements for dimensions.
other_countries_reviews.text: I'm flying with my cat so I had to shop for a pet carrier that is according to the Air Canada guide line. This is the best pet carrier I can find..It has lots of room for my cat to move around. The top and sides can expand. He is tall but with the top extension he can sit comfortably.It feels heavier than other carrier but I plan to use my carry on to wheel him around the airport.
other_countries_reviews.author: Nati

other_countries_reviews.title: Great for airplane travel
other_countries_reviews.text: Worked well for traveling with a cat. Fit nicely under the seat on the airplane.
other_countries_reviews.author: Corey
